prose:
Electrostatic dipole moment of acetonitrile

Method provenance block
Property: molecular dipole moment (|μ|)
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding)
Geometry provenance: fixed input geometry taken from /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/electrostatics-validation/acetonitrile/acetonitrile.xyz; no optimization performed (single-point electrostatics calculation)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: xtb via chemkit electrostatics skill (chemkit engine backend xtb 6.x series)
Convergence & caveats: SCF achieved; integrity check status OK. No warnings emitted by the backend. Density-fitting is intrinsic to GFN2-xTB; the method is appropriate for dipole magnitudes but absolute energies are semi-empirical.
Live output: the engine streamed stdout/stderr to a live log generated by chemkit (e.g. electrostatics_*.out) in the caller’s working directory while running; it can be inspected for full SCF details. The JSON result file is archived at the path given in cli_invocation.

Headline result
Dipole moment |μ| = 3.87205 D
